topic.what: Protocol-aware security testing framework for MCP tool servers exposing developer pitfalls under multi-vector attack conditions
topic.how: Uses Tier-1 static analysis with trace-grounded validation and objective validators (Vconf/Vint) to detect P1-P6 pitfall classes and measure mitigation effectiveness
topic.why_matters: MCP reached 97M+ monthly downloads but secure deployment is error-prone; existing benchmarks lack remediation guidance and rely on agent self-report which diverges from trace evidence in 63.2% of runs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Precision-recall F1 score on statically checkable pitfall classes P1/P2/P5/P6
characteristics.evidence: Table 2: 36 binary labels, 12 TP, 0 FP, 0 FN for these classes
characteristics.context: Tier-1 static analysis over 6 server variants

characteristics.what_it_measures: Recall for trace/dataflow-dependent pitfall classes P3/P4
characteristics.vs_baseline: false negatives indicate dataflow-dependent pitfalls require Tier-2 analysis
characteristics.evidence: Table 2: 0 TP, 0 FP, 2 FN each for P3 and P4
characteristics.context: P3/P4 require inter-procedural or trace-aware analysis

characteristics.what_it_measures: Framework risk score difference between baseline and hardened servers
characteristics.vs_baseline: 29 Tier-1 findings (16 HIGH, 13 MEDIUM) reduced to 0
characteristics.evidence: Table 3: risk reduced from 10.0 to 0.0 across all 3 scenarios
characteristics.context: Risk = min(10, sum of weighted severity findings)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Lines of non-comment code added for hardening per server
characteristics.evidence: Table 4: mean 27 LOC (15 docsystem, 32 emailsystem, 35 ETHPriceServer)
characteristics.context: Small localized changes yield disproportionate risk reduction

characteristics.what_itasures: Risk reduction normalized by implementation effort
characteristics.evidence: Table 4: mean CE 4.17 (3.99 email, 4.60 doc, 3.93 ETH)
characteristics.context: Demonstrates actionable vs aspirational security guidance

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of runs where agent narrative diverges from protocol trace
characteristics.vs_baseline: All divergences are D5 sink misattribution (MEDIUM severity)
characteristics.evidence: Table 6: 12/19 runs show divergence; 12/12 sink-action runs affected
characteristics.context: Preliminary 19-run corpus from emailsystem (tool poisoning + puppet)

characteristics.what_itasures: Execution time for Tier-1 static analyzer
characteristics.context: Enables pre-commit hooks and CI pipeline integration

characteristics.what_it_measures: Audit logging coverage increase after hardening
characteristics.vs_baseline: Baseline variants lacked consistent audit logging
characteristics.context: Structured argument-bearing logs for high-risk actions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Server-side validation coverage increase after hardening
characteristics.vs_baseline: Added allowlists, ValueError guards, recipient validation
characteristics.context: Server-side enforcement vs agent self-restriction assumption
